channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Facebook Groups are fueling a black market for Uber and DoorDash accounts, says a new report
item.description: A new watchdog report uncovers Facebook groups quietly fueling a black market for operational Uber and DoorDash accounts, posing serious safety concerns for riders and customers.
item.dc:creator: Arden Anderson, Fast Company

item.title: Smartphones may boost children's mental health, study finds
item.description: The study's findings challenge the assumption that kids who own smartphones tend to fare worse than those who don't.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Over 1.6 million customers now hit in massive insurance data breach - how to check if you're one of them
item.description: Initial reports said around 800,000 individuals were affected, but real number is now thought to be double that.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I’ve never seen so many bug fixes in an Nvidia GPU driver, as new release offers vital cures for black screen crashes, Windows 11 24H2 glitches and more
item.description: Nvidia only talks about fixing black screen woes for RTX 5000 graphics cards, so what about past-gen GPUs? Fortunately, it seems like good news here, too.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: CIOs must mind their own data confidence gap
item.description: As far as many C-suite business and IT executives are concerned, their company data is in great shape, capable of fueling data-driven decision-making and delivering AI-powered solutions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Forget AI, children still need to learn how to code, Github CEO says - here's how you can get them to start
item.description: GitHub CEO squashes job certainty concerns by stating that children should be learning to code from a young age.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The 'final' One UI 7 update is rolling out to the Galaxy S25, but older phones could still be waiting a while to get it
item.description: There's still a lack of clarity around when handsets such as the Galaxy S23 might finally get One UI 7.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I CIO stanno spendendo troppo per il cloud, ma pensano ancora che ne valga la pena
item.description: I notevoli aumenti imprevisti della spesa per il cloud, dovuti in gran parte a un maggiore utilizzo, non stanno spingendo i CIO a ripensare le strategie IT al di là dei cambiamenti tattici nel tentativo di contenere i costi del cloud.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Gleam 1.10 improves compiler, JavaScript codegen
item.description: Gleam 1.10, a new release of the type-safe programming language for the Erlang virtual machine and JavaScript runtimes, is now available. The update features compiler improvements centering on holding more information and improving exhaustive analysis.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cyber roundup: CISA pulls CVE from the brink
item.description: CISA extended the CVE program for now, but the cyber agency is being accused of playing "a game of chicken" with its funding. The post Cyber roundup: CISA pulls CVE from the brink first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A colossal squid is caught on camera for the first time in the deep sea
item.description: A colossal squid has been captured on camera for the first time in the deep sea by an international team of researchers steering a remotely operated submersible.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Transforming customer experience with AI at Alorica
item.description: Alorica, a global leader in customer experience solutions serving Fortune 500 companies, has positioned itself at the forefront of AI innovation in customer service, developing solutions that directly address client needs while elevating agent capabilities.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Justice Department prohibits employees from sharing anything related to their work on social media
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has ordered U.S. Justice Department employees not to post anything on social media related to their government work, after a wave of new political appointees took to cheering Trump and castigating his opponents online. The directive, which was emailed to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Major U.S. banks pause data sharing with this federal bureau after a cyberattack exposed sensitive information
item.description: Several of the largest U.S. banks are reportedly pausing or reassessing how they send sensitive information to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) following a major cyberattack on the regulator.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: DHS cancels federal neurodiversity workforce contract
item.description: DHS did not provide a reason for cancelling CISA's neurodiversity workforce pilot, but it came amid the Trump administration's anti-DEI push. The post DHS cancels federal neurodiversity workforce contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Fewer Americans want government, tech companies restricting false content online: Poll
item.description: Support for the U.S. government or technology companies' attempts to restrict false information online dropped slightly among Americans over the past two years, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How to keep SMBs open for business – even when the power goes out
item.description: Internet outages are bad news for small- to medium-sized businesses. Point-of-sale systems won’t work when connections fail, meaning transactions can’t go through and revenue is lost until service is restored.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers call on National Archives to investigate use of Signal and Gmail by top government officials
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee say the reported use of Signal and Gmail by national security officials creates major cybersecurity risks.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How pet projects fuel innovation and careers in tech
item.description: Call them pet projects, side projects, or hobby projects, projects pursued outside of one’s regular business activity are especially popular among software developers. After all, these projects serve as an excellent way to learn new skills and technologies or to test fresh concepts and approaches.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vector Institute aims to clear up confusion about AI model performance
item.description: AI models are advancing at a dizzying clip, with builders boasting ever more impressive performance with each iteration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
